Let me understand what happened with SPACE:
- The bottom container background (id=21) went from 576 pixels to 1, and the charcoal area (id=22) expanded to fill it (576 pixels)
- Bottom squares shifted: the first one (green) was removed, and the rest shifted left with a new yellow appearing at the end
- Current bottom order: green, purple, blue, yellow (ids 23-26)
- Wait, looking at the boundaries, the squares are still at the same positions. Let me re-read:

  - id=24: p (purple) at [57, 26]-[60, 29] - same position as before for id=23
  - id=25: b (blue) at [57, 34]-[60, 37] - same position as before for id=24
  - id=26: Y (yellow) at [57, 42]-[60, 45] - same position as before for id=25
So the colors shifted left: position 1 got green (was green), position 2 got purple (was purple), position 3 got blue (was blue), position 4 got yellow (was yellow). Wait, that's the same order!
Actually, let me re-check. Before SPACE:
- id=22: N (green) at cols 18-21
- id=23: p (purple) at cols 26-29
- id=24: b (blue) at cols 34-37

- id=24: p (purple) at cols 26-29
- id=25: b (blue) at cols 34-37
- id=26: Y (yellow) at cols 42-45
The colors are the same! The only changes were:
1. Separator lost 1 pixel (timer countdown)
2. Container background changed from c to G (1 pixel)
3. The first square (id=22) changed from N to c and grew to fill the container
4. New node id=26 appeared
Wait, but the diff said:
- Node 22 changed: color N->c pixels 16->576 (the green square became the background)
- Node 23 changed: color p->N pixels 16->16 (purple became green)
- Node 24 changed: color b->p pixels 16->16 (blue became purple)
- Node 25 changed: color Y->b pixels 16->16 (yellow became blue)
- Node 26 appeared: color=Y pixels=16 (new yellow)
So the bottom row colors shifted right! Before: [green, purple, blue, yellow]. After: [green, purple, blue, yellow]... wait no.
Let me trace this more carefully. The nodes keep their positions but change colors:
- Position 1 (cols 18-21): was green (N), now is charcoal (c) - but wait, node 22 grew to 576 pixels which is the whole container. So the green square disappeared and became the background.
- Position 2 (cols 26-29): was purple (p), now is green (N) 
- Position 3 (cols 34-37): was blue (b), now is purple (p)
- Position 4 (cols 42-45): was yellow (Y), now is blue (b)
- New position 5 (appears at cols 42-45): yellow (Y)
Hmm wait, positions 4 and 5 overlap? No, let me check. Node 25 is now blue at cols 34-37, and node 26 is yellow at cols 42-45. But node 25 was originally yellow at cols 42-45...
I think the issue is that node IDs don't track positions - they're assigned by top-left order. Let me look at the boundaries more carefully.
After SPACE:
- id=22: c, 576px, boundary [[54, 0], [54, 63], [63, 63], [63, 0]] - this is the entire bottom container area

- id=25: Y (yellow), 16px, [[57, 42], [57, 45], [60, 45], [60, 42]]
So the colors at each position:
Before: [green, purple, blue, yellow]
After: [green, purple, blue, yellow]
They're the same! The only real change is:
1. The container background (id=21) changed from charcoal to dark gray, shrinking to 1 pixel
2. id=22 expanded from a green square to the full container background
3. A new node id=26 appeared (yellow at the same position as before)
Wait, this doesn't make sense. The colors at each position are identical. Let me reconsider...
Actually, I think what happened is:
- The background of the bottom panel changed from charcoal (c) to dark gray (G)
- The old background (id=21, c, 576px) became id=22 (c, 576px) 
- The new background is id=21 (G, 1px)
- The colored squares kept their colors and positions
So the actual gameplay change might just be the timer (separator line lost 1 pixel) and some background color shift. The bottom squares didn't actually change order.
Hmm, but the diff clearly showed color changes for nodes 22-25. Let me reconsider...
